data.title: Spocket New Features: US & EU Dropshipping Just Leveled Up
data.excerpt: Spocket just launched powerful new features for US & EU dropshipping. See what's changed, who it's for, and why 15K+ merchants swear by it. Try it now.

$media.alt: Spocket New Features: US & EU Dropshipping Just Leveled Up

children.text: If you've been running a Shopify or WooCommerce store for any length of time, you already know the dropshipping supplier hunt is the single biggest time drain in your business. Sifting through AliExpress listings, wondering if that "US warehouse" label is real, dealing with three-week shipping times — it's exhausting.

Spocket has been solving that problem for years. But their latest round of updates takes the platform from "really useful" to "I can't believe this is still priced the same." I spent the last two weeks digging through every new feature, and here's the full breakdown.
*Disclosure: Some links in this post are affiliate links. I only recommend tools I've genuinely evaluated and believe deliver real value.*
Why Spocket Already Stands Out in the Dropshipping Space
Before we get into the new stuff, it's worth understanding why Spocket consistently ranks as the
number one dropshipping app on Shopify
with over 15,000 reviews.
The core value proposition is simple but powerful:
Spocket connects you to suppliers based in the United States and Europe

, not just China. That one distinction changes everything about the customer experience — shipping times drop from 15–30 days to 2–7 days, product quality gets a serious upgrade, and returns become manageable instead of nightmarish.

The marketplace currently features over 20 million products across virtually every category you can imagine: fashion, electronics, home goods, beauty, fitness, pet supplies, jewelry, and niche items you won't find on the typical AliExpress scroll.
What makes Spocket different from just finding a domestic supplier on your own? Three things:
One-click import
directly into your Shopify or WooCommerce store — product descriptions, images, variants, and pricing rules all sync automatically.
Branded invoicing
so your customers see your brand, not a random supplier's name on the package.
Real-time inventory sync
that prevents you from selling out-of-stock items and dealing with angry customer emails.
These aren't new features. They've been Spocket's foundation for years. But the team clearly hasn't been resting.
New and Highlighted Features You'll Actually Use
This is where things get exciting. Spocket's recent updates address the exact pain points I hear about most from dropshippers. Here's what's changed.
Winning Products Catalog Expansion

Spocket now boasts a curated catalog of over 20 million products, but the real upgrade is in how they surface *winning* items. Their improved algorithm analyzes trending data, margin potential, and supplier reliability scores to push high-converting products to the top of your search results.

Instead of manually testing dozens of products hoping something sticks, you can now browse categories filtered by profit margin range, shipping speed, and supplier location. For anyone who's spent hours split-testing AliExpress products with no results, this feature alone is worth the subscription.
Enhanced Supplier Vetting and Verification

One of the biggest concerns in dropshipping is supplier legitimacy. Spocket has doubled down on their verification process. Every supplier in the marketplace now goes through a multi-step quality check that includes:
Sample order fulfillment
— Spocket actually orders products to test quality and shipping speed.
Response time monitoring
— suppliers who don't respond to retailer questions within 24 hours get flagged.
Customer satisfaction scoring
— based on actual order outcomes, not just self-reported metrics.

This means when you import a product into your store, you're working with a supplier who has already proven they can deliver. That's a massive confidence boost, especially when you're scaling past your first hundred orders.
AI-Powered Store Building

This one caught me off guard. Spocket now offers an AI store builder that can generate a functional Shopify store in about one minute. You answer a few questions about your niche and preferences, and the tool builds out a storefront with pre-loaded products, organized categories, and basic branding.

Is it going to replace a custom-designed store built by a professional? No. But for someone who's been sitting on a business idea for months and hasn't launched because the setup felt overwhelming, this removes the biggest barrier to entry. You can go from zero to a live store with real products in an afternoon.
If you want a deep dive into setup, check out our
step-by-step Spocket tutorial
Improved Pricing and Discount Tools

Spocket's new pricing dashboard gives you clearer visibility into your margins per product. You can set global markup rules (e.g., "always price at 2.5x supplier cost") or customize pricing per item. The update also includes a
compare-at price
feature that lets you show a crossed-out original price in your store, which — let's be honest — is one of the oldest conversion tricks in ecommerce for a reason. It works.
Faster Sync and Reliability Updates

Backend performance has improved noticeably. Product imports that used to take 10–15 seconds now happen in 3–5 seconds. Inventory updates happen in near-real-time instead of the previous batch cycle. For stores processing 50+ orders per day, this kind of reliability matters because every minute of outdated stock data is a potential refund request.
Ready to explore the marketplace yourself?
Start browsing Spocket's product catalog here
Who Is Spocket Ideal For?
Spocket isn't for everyone, and that's by design. Here's who gets the most value from the platform:
First-Time Store Owners

If you've never built an online store before, Spocket removes about 60% of the usual headaches. The supplier vetting, one-click imports, and AI store builder mean you can focus on marketing and customer experience instead of logistics. You don't need to negotiate with suppliers, manage inventory, or figure out shipping carriers. Spocket handles the operational side so you can handle the creative side.
Shopify and WooCommerce Users Looking to Upgrade from AliExpress

If you're currently dropshipping from AliExpress and dealing with long shipping times, inconsistent quality, and customer complaints, switching to Spocket's US and EU suppliers is the single highest-ROI move you can make. Your customers get their orders faster, your refund rate drops, and your reviews improve. It's that straightforward.
Niche Store Operators

Spocket's filtering tools make it easy to go deep into a specific category. Whether you're selling minimalist home decor, organic skincare, or premium pet accessories, you can find suppliers who specialize in that niche rather than sourcing from generic mega-suppliers.
Experienced Sellers Scaling to Multiple Stores

For entrepreneurs running 2–5 stores (or more), Spocket's bulk import features and centralized dashboard make management practical. You can apply different pricing rules per store, manage separate product catalogs, and monitor supplier performance across all your businesses from one login.
Agency Owners and Freelancers Building Stores for Clients

If you build Shopify stores as a service, Spocket lets you populate a client's store with quality products in minutes instead of hours. The branded invoicing feature means your client's customers never see the supplier's name — it looks like the client is fulfilling everything themselves.
Want to see how others are using the platform? Our guide on
Spocket use cases
covers real scenarios in detail.
Explore Spocket's full feature set and pricing
— they offer a free trial so you can test everything before committing.
How to Get Started With Spocket Today
Getting started is genuinely fast. Here's the process:
Sign up for a free Spocket account
— no credit card required for the trial.
Connect your store
— Spocket integrates natively with Shopify and WooCommerce. The connection takes about 60 seconds.
Browse the marketplace
— use filters for supplier location, category, price range, and shipping speed.
Import products
— click the "Add to Import List" button on any product, customize your pricing and description, then push it live to your store.
Start selling
— when a customer places an order, Spocket automatically routes it to the supplier who ships directly to your customer.
That's the simplified version. For the full walkthrough with screenshots and troubleshooting tips, bookmark our
Spocket getting-started guide
Pro Tips to Maximize Your Spocket Experience
After spending serious time with the platform, here are the strategies that separate successful Spocket users from frustrated ones:
Order samples before going live.

Even with Spocket's vetting, you should always order a sample of your top 3–5 products. Test the packaging, shipping speed, and product quality yourself. This takes a few days and a few dollars but prevents you from marketing a product that disappoints.
Use the profit margin filter aggressively.

Don't waste time importing products with razor-thin margins. Filter for items with at least a 40–50% margin after accounting for your marketing costs. Spocket's new pricing dashboard makes this calculation easy.
Leverage branded invoicing from day one.
Turn this on immediately. It's a small detail that builds brand trust and makes your store feel professional, not like a middleman operation.
Set up automatic pricing rules early.
Manually pricing 200 products is soul-crushing work. Use Spocket's global markup rules and only customize pricing for your hero products.
Monitor supplier scores weekly.
Spocket shows supplier performance metrics. If a supplier's score drops, switch to an alternative before it affects your customer experience.
Pair Spocket with strong project management.
Running an ecommerce store involves dozens of moving parts — marketing campaigns, inventory decisions, customer service, content creation. Tools like
ClickUp
or
monday.com
help you keep everything organized as you scale.
For more advanced tactics, read our complete collection of
Spocket tips and best practices
The Bottom Line: Is Spocket Worth It in 2025?

Absolutely. The combination of US and EU suppliers, real-time inventory sync, branded invoicing, AI-powered store building, and a curated winning products catalog makes Spocket the most complete dropshipping platform available for Shopify and WooCommerce merchants.
The new features haven't added complexity — they've removed friction. That's the sign of a product team that actually listens to its users.

Whether you're launching your first store or optimizing your fifth, Spocket gives you the supplier infrastructure to compete with brands that hold their own inventory. And you get to do it without the upfront cost, warehouse headaches, or risk of buying bulk stock that doesn't sell.
If you're serious about building a dropshipping business that customers actually trust and return to,
start your free Spocket trial today
. You'll see the difference in your first week.

A laptop screen showing a clean dropshipping dashboard with product cards from US and EU suppliers, surrounded by small cardboard shipping boxes and a coffee cup on a minimalist white desk. Glassmorphism cards, soft volumetric light, generous negative space for a title, no embedded text, no watermark. --ar 16:9 --style raw

children.text: Is Spocket free to use?

Spocket offers a free trial so you can explore the marketplace, connect your store, and test the import workflow without entering payment details. Paid plans unlock access to premium suppliers, higher product limits, and advanced features like branded invoicing and the winning products catalog.
Can I use Spocket with WooCommerce, or is it Shopify-only?

Spocket integrates natively with both Shopify and WooCommerce. The setup process is nearly identical for both platforms — you install the Spocket app or plugin, authorize the connection, and start importing products immediately.
How fast is shipping with Spocket's US and EU suppliers?

Most products from US and EU suppliers ship within 2–7 days domestically, which is dramatically faster than the 15–30 day timelines common with AliExpress suppliers. Exact delivery times depend on the supplier's location relative to your customer, but the improvement over China-based fulfillment is consistent and significant.
Does Spocket support branded invoicing?

Yes. Spocket's branded invoicing feature lets you customize the invoice that ships with each order so it displays your store's name and branding instead of the supplier's. This creates a seamless customer experience and strengthens your brand identity with every shipment.
